title: Grid Identity, Coordinates, Geometry, and Units · Program Brief: Geometry Units
summary: Rows and columns are mixed with pixels, the y-axis is assumed to point upward, resize changes cell identity, and rounding leaves gaps. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the geometry units increment in typed logical-grid and viewport geometry model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Name cells, pixels, logical units, scale factors, padding, and line widths in every geometry formula.
Convert between row-column, linear index, logical point, and Canvas coordinates with explicit origins and axis direction.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: maze-geometry-units — Name cells, pixels, logical units, scale factors, padding, and line widths in every geometry formula.
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-grid-coordinate-model-4
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-grid-coordinate-model-4.
# Case 1655: a classroom algorithm comparison.
# Operating constraint: native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution.
# Changed case: change display scale and retain focus and control access.
def worked_maze_grid_coordinate_model_4(rows: int = 6, columns: int = 8, cell_size: int = 24, mapped_cells: int = 48, round_trips: int = 48) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "gridworked4",
        "topology1655",
        "search4dekrow",
        "eventworked41655",
        "access1655worked4",
        "recovery4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if rows < 1 or columns < 1 or cell_size < 1:
        return False, mapped_cells, "geometry-shape"
    if mapped_cells != rows * columns:
        return False, mapped_cells, "cell-identity"
    if round_trips != mapped_cells:
        return False, round_trips, "coordinate-round-trip"
    if rows * columns > 1000000:
        return False, mapped_cells, "grid-budget"
    assert mapped_cells == rows * columns
    return True, mapped_cells, "bounded-grid-geometry"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Name cells, pixels, logical units, scale factors, padding, and line widths in every geometry formula.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Geometry Units model · Theory Geometry Units, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Geometry Units precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Geometry Units model · Theory Geometry Units, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 1254 during a low-vision player session. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile logical grid identity, viewport geometry, units, and coordinate round trips; native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution; then switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Name cells, pixels, logical units, scale factors, padding, and line widths in every geometry formula.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Geometry Units model · Theory Geometry Units, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Numbers used in geometry are interchangeable when magnitudes look plausible.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: maze-geometry-units-1jjgs2(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_maze_geometry_units_1jjgs2\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?rows < 1 or columns < 1 or cell_size < 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?mapped_cells != rows \* columns)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?round_trips != mapped_cells)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?rows \* columns > 1000000)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
